message:
fix(round): use round-half-away-from-zero matching SQLite custom printf (#96) + 24 conformance tests

SQLite's custom printf uses "round half away from zero" for round(X,N),
while Rust's format!() uses IEEE 754 "round half to even". They agree
on all cases except exact ties (e.g. round(2.25,1): 2.3 vs 2.2).

Fix: format with extra precision to extract true decimal digits, detect
exact ties (rounding digit==5 with all-zero trailing), and manually
increment the truncated string for tie cases. Applied to both
fsqlite-func::RoundFunc and connection.rs eval_scalar_fn.

24 new conformance oracle tests (s243-s266) covering: CHECK constraints,
AUTOINCREMENT, DEFAULT values, COALESCE chains, BETWEEN, EXCEPT/INTERSECT,
ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN, REPLACE INTO, INSERT OR REPLACE, GROUP_CONCAT,
multi-table DELETE, nested CASE, rowid aliases, math functions, CAST,
ORDER BY nulls, multi-column UNIQUE, UPDATE subqueries, count(*) vs
count(col), CROSS JOIN, string functions, INSERT...SELECT, complex WHERE.

663 pass, 5 pre-existing type-coercion fails, 7 ignored.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
